mnemonics.title: CSP Components
mnemonics.content: CSP: Variables, Domains, Constraints - VDC (Remember: Variables Define Choices)

mnemonics.title: Backtracking Order
mnemonics.content: SELECT → ASSIGN → CHECK → RECURSE or BACKTRACK - Remember: SARC (like 'shark')

mnemonics.title: Heuristics Remembered
mnemonics.content:
MRV: Most Restricted Variable (fewest choices first)
LCV: Least Constraining Value (most flexible first)
Degree: Most connected first - Think: 'Most constrained, most flexible, most connected'

acronyms.usage: Heuristic selecting variable with smallest domain size to fail early
Heuristic preferring values that restrict fewer choices for neighbors
Problem defined by variables, domains, and constraints to satisfy
Algorithm ensuring binary constraints are consistent via domain pruning
Backtracking with AC-3 run after each assignment for strong propagation

visualTips.visualization: Draw variables as circles/nodes, binary constraints as arrows/edges connecting them. For N-Queens, visualize diagonals as diagonal lines crossing the board.
Show domains as lists next to each variable. When constraint is applied, cross out invalid values. Use elimination diagram showing crossed-out values.
Draw search tree with root at top. Branch down for each value tried. Show backtracking as returning up the tree to try alternative branch.
For arc (X→Y), draw X's domain values and Y's supporting values. If X has value with no support in Y, draw X through it to remove.

analogies.analogy: Like filling a Sudoku puzzle - you pick a cell (variable), try a number (value), check if it works with existing numbers (constraints), and if conflict occurs, erase and try another (backtrack).
Like packing a suitcase - when you decide to pack an item, you immediately remove incompatible items from consideration, rather than discovering conflicts later.
Like solving a maze by going through the narrowest corridor first - if it dead-ends, you'll know immediately rather than wasting time in wider paths.
Like weeding a garden - removing obviously dead options (inconsistent values) before planting (searching), making the remaining work easier.
